message:
🌱 Add kube-rs: Create a Controller<K> runtime mission (#1008)

Signed-off-by: Andy Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,77 @@
+{
+  "version": "kc-mission-v1",
+  "name": "kube-rs-148-create-a-controller-k-runtime",
+  "missionClass": "solution",
+  "author": "KubeStellar Bot",
+  "authorGithub": "kubestellar",
+  "mission": {
+    "title": "kube-rs: Create a Controller<K> runtime",
+    "description": "Create a Controller<K> runtime. Community-requested feature.",
+    "type": "feature",
+    "status": "completed",
+    "steps": [
+      {
+        "title": "Check current kube-rs project",
+        "description": "Verify your kube-rs version and configuration:\n```bash\ncargo --version\nrustc --version\ngrep kube Cargo.toml 2>/dev/null\n```\nThis feature requires a Rust development environment with the kube-rs crate as a dependency."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Review kube-rs configuration",
+        "description": "Inspect the relevant kube-rs configuration:\n```bash\ngrep -A5 '\\[dependencies\\]' Cargo.toml\ncargo tree | grep kube\n```\nTo provide something like [controller-runtime](https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/controller-runtime/tree/master/examples/crd)'s `Controller`s."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Apply the fix for Create a Controller<K> runtime",
+        "description": "Sketch of Controller<T>. Heavy WIP for #148 \n\nSome thoughts:\n- using a pull based model like  in Informer because it feels nicer than callbacks, and is consistent\n- probably will use some kind of signalling to ensure we can debounce events and notify the owner of what does the parallel poll when it should release an item into the stream\n\nSee the source issue for community-verified solutions."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Verify the feature works",
+        "description": "Test that the new capability is working as expected:\n```bash\ncargo build 2>&1 | tail -20\ncargo test 2>&1 | tail -20\n```\nConfirm the feature described in \"Create a Controller<K> runtime\" is functioning correctly."
+      }
+    ],
+    "resolution": {
+      "summary": "Sketch of Controller<T>. Heavy WIP for #148 \n\nSome thoughts:\n- using a pull based model like  in Informer because it feels nicer than callbacks, and is consistent\n- probably will use some kind of signalling to ensure we can debounce events and notify the owner of what does the parallel poll when it should release an item into the stream",
+      "codeSnippets": []
+    }
+  },
